title: I am Thine, O Lord, I have heard Thy voice
firstLine: I am Thine, O Lord, I have heard Thy voice,
publicDomainBasis: Fanny Jane Crosby (1820–1915), William Howard Doane (1832–1915). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
I am Thine, O Lord, I have heard Thy voice,
And it told Thy love to me;
But I long to rise in the arms of faith,
And be closer drawn to Thee.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Consecrate me now to Thy service, Lord,
By the pow’r of grace divine;
Let my soul look up with a steadfast hope,
And my will be lost in Thine.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
O the pure delight of a single hour
That before Thy throne I spend,
When I kneel in prayer, and with Thee, my God,
I commune as friend with friend!

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
There are depths of love that I yet may know
Ere Thee face to face I see;
There are heights of joy that I yet may reach
Ere I rest in peace with Thee.
